note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Introduction to Drying Educational and Institutional Sites — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: In a laboratory, which feature does the lesson say must be protected from moisture because corrosion can compromise its ability to remove hazardous substances from the air?
In auditoriums, which components does the lesson identify as particularly susceptible to rust and requiring specialised climate control around them?
options: Soundboards, lighting arrays and orchestra shell hydraulics
Fibre optic backbones and routing equipment

questions.questionText: When relocating flood-threatened archives and collections, the lesson recommends coordinating relocation logistics with which specialists?
questions.sourceRef: M1 — Special Design Features (Archives and Collections)

questions.questionText: According to the lesson, what must be maintained on a facility's fire protection system even if repairs to the water mains, post indicator valves or fire pumps take months?
To manage the unpredictable power demands of running drying equipment and facility electronics simultaneously for months, the lesson recommends establishing what?
When relocating athletics dormitories during drying, which occupants does the lesson single out as particularly vulnerable to mould exposure and off-gassed volatile organic compounds (VOCs)?
When federal grant funds are used to purchase drying equipment, what does the lesson say facilities should retain to demonstrate the equipment's utilisation and justify the expenditure?
What does the incremental drying strategy described in the lesson involve doing to affected wings?
options: Leasing modular classroom trailers for the whole wing
Installing a chilled water piping bypass to each room
Relocating the entire wing to interim facilities at once
Dividing them into compartmentalised drying zones renovated on a staggered basis
